market.summary: Global art market demonstrates cautious optimism in early 2026, recovering from 2025 growth of 4% ($59.6 billion). Recovery is uneven, with strength concentrated in high-end ($10M+) segments.
keyTrends: Shift towards high-quality, rarity-driven supply; decrease in new talent representation at auctions
U.S. market retains dominance with 44% of global sales
Increased risk aversion: guarantee value share at NYC sales reached 78%
Emergence of 'Hyper-Tactility' in art styles and continued growth of digital art

highlights: Market rebound driven by 9% growth in public auction sales
Impressionist and Modern art segments leading historical category recovery
Private sales declined 5%, prompting dealers to adopt hybrid business models
Geopolitical and economic uncertainty remains the primary concern for dealers
